title: Patio citrus candle
subtitle: A soy wax outdoor candle with lemon and eucalyptus for the garden table
excerpt: A soy wax container candle with lemon and eucalyptus essential oils for outdoor use on a garden table or patio. The citrus and eucalyptus combination is commonly used as a natural deterrent for mosquitoes. Burns approximately 25-30 hours.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry outdoor candle guide (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): fragrance load for soy in outdoor conditions. WHO report on plant-based mosquito repellents (WHO/CDS/WHOPES/2001.5): essential oils with insect-deterrent properties noted in scientific literature.

content.text: A soy wax jar candle for outdoor summer evenings. Lemon and eucalyptus essential oils give it a clean, refreshing scent. Both oils are known plant-based deterrents for mosquitoes and midges. At this dilution the candle is a light deterrent, not a strong one. The main job is the scent on the garden table.
Lemon essential oil has a low

(around 46°C) and is phototoxic when applied to skin exposed to direct sunlight; in a candle it stays in the wax and poses no phototoxicity risk. Eucalyptus essential oil is toxic to cats; label the candle clearly if used around pets. Melt wax over a simmering-water bath. Never leave melting wax unattended.

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: 4% of wax weight. Cold-pressed lemon peel oil. Add at 55°C.

items.name: Eucalyptus essential oil
items.prepNote: 2% of wax weight. Eucalyptus globulus. Toxic to cats. Add alongside the lemon EO.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: CD-12 or ECO-8 for a 7 cm diameter jar.

content.text: Set the wick and melt the wax
Secure the wick tab to the jar base. Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over a simmering-water bath. Allow to cool to 55°C.

Combine the lemon and eucalyptus essential oils in a small dish, then add the blend to the wax at 55°C. Stir gently for 2 minutes. Pour into the prepared jar and allow to set undisturbed for 24 hours.

indoors in still air, allowing 3-4 hours for the full melt pool to form before extinguishing. Outdoors, a breeze deflects the flame and prevents the melt pool from forming fully. Trim the wick to 5 mm before each use.

Store the candle indoors in a cool, dark place between uses. Outdoor storage in direct sun will fade the wax colour and the fragrance. Lemon EO fades after 12-18 months; the eucalyptus note holds longer. Discard if the candle smells rancid or the wax surface is discoloured.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (~25-30 burn hours)
